strInstructions: Make the sate by combining chili flakes, water, fish sauce, and sugar, then frying it with annatto oil, minced lemongrass, shallots, and garlic until thick and fragrant. Prepare the pickled shallots and carrots with vinegar, sugar, and salt. Make the crab meatballs by mixing crab meat, pork paste, fried aromatics, annatto oil, pepper, and stock powder. For the broth, simmer pork hock, beef bones, beef shank or brisket, lemongrass, rock sugar, fish sauce, shrimp paste, salt, and stock powder until rich. Cook thick rice noodles separately. Serve the noodles with broth, sliced beef, pork hock, crab meatballs, herbs, scallions, onion, pickled vegetables, and sate.
strMeasure13: 10 g for sate plus 10 pieces for pickles

strInstructions: In a large bowl, dissolve rice flour, turmeric powder, and salt in water. Add beer or coconut milk and chopped spring onion, then let the batter rest. Season pork belly and shrimp with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a heavy skillet, add shrimp and pork, then ladle in batter and swirl to spread thinly. Cover briefly, add bean sprouts, then cook until crisp. Fold the crepe in half and repeat with the remaining batter. Serve with lettuce, herbs, and dipping fish sauce.

Blanch pork belly with salt and vinegar, rinse, then simmer until cooked. Cool and slice thinly. Boil, steam, or dry-fry shrimp until orange, then peel, devein, and halve lengthwise. Cook rice vermicelli and prepare lettuce, cucumber, mint, and coriander. Make the dipping sauce by sautéing garlic, then adding hoisin sauce, sugar, pork broth, and peanut butter. Wet rice paper sheets, layer lettuce, vermicelli, herbs, cucumber, pork, and shrimp, then fold and roll tightly. Serve with peanut hoisin sauce, crushed peanuts, garlic, and chili.

Prepare the batter with rice flour, tapioca starch, water, salt, and oil, then let it rest. Cook the filling by sautéing ground pork with wood ear mushrooms, onion, shallots, fish sauce, salt, and pepper. Pour a thin layer of batter onto a nonstick pan or steamer, cover briefly, then transfer the delicate rice sheet to a plate. Add pork filling and roll. Serve with Vietnamese pork sausage, fried shallots, fresh herbs, cucumber, bean sprouts, and dipping fish sauce.

Soak peeled split mung beans and sticky rice separately. Cook the mung beans until soft, then mash or shape into a firm block. Mix sticky rice with turmeric powder, salt, and pandan leaves, then steam until tender. Fry thinly sliced shallots in oil until golden and crisp. Serve sticky rice topped with shaved mung bean, fried shallots, shallot oil, sugar, and salt.

Char onion and ginger until fragrant. Toast spices such as star anise, cinnamon, cloves, coriander seeds, and cardamom. Simmer chicken with the charred aromatics and toasted spices until the meat is cooked and the broth is clear and flavorful. Remove the chicken, shred or slice the meat, then season the broth with fish sauce, salt, and rock sugar. Cook rice noodles separately. Serve noodles with chicken, hot broth, onion, herbs, bean sprouts, lime, and chili.

Prepare a pork and dried shrimp broth, then season with fish sauce, salt, sugar, and tomato. Mix crab paste, ground pork, eggs, dried shrimp, and seasonings to form the crab mixture. Add the mixture gently to the simmering broth so it forms soft crab cakes. Add fried tofu and tomatoes. Cook rice vermicelli separately, then serve with broth, crab mixture, tofu, herbs, lime, and chili.

Marinate the chicken with fish sauce, turmeric, garlic, shallots, and seasonings. Sauté aromatics until fragrant, then add the marinated chicken and cook until coated. Add broth and simmer until the chicken is cooked and the broth is flavorful. Cook the turmeric rice noodles separately. Place noodles in bowls, add chicken and a small amount of broth, then top with fresh herbs, lettuce, banana blossom, crushed peanuts, sesame rice crackers, lime, and chili.

Clean the pork, pat it dry, then marinate with five-spice powder, sugar, pepper, garlic, soy sauce, garlic chili sauce, and chicken powder. Let it marinate for at least 1 hour. Cook the pork until tender and flavorful, reserving the cooking liquid as the sauce. Prepare thick rice noodles, bean sprouts, lettuce, herbs, and crispy noodles. Assemble the bowl with noodles, sliced pork, herbs, bean sprouts, lettuce, crispy topping, and a small amount of the pork sauce.

Mix rice flour, tapioca flour, salt, water, and vegetable oil until smooth, then let the batter rest. Soak dried salted shrimp, then process or pound until fluffy. Steam small bowls, pour a thin layer of batter into each bowl, and steam until the rice cakes are set. Top each cake with dried shrimp, mung bean paste, scallion oil, fried pork fat or fried shallots, and serve with Vietnamese dipping sauce.

Marinate beef with bo kho spice, fish sauce, sugar, garlic, ginger, lemongrass, and annatto oil. Brown the beef, then add tomato paste, coconut water or broth, star anise, cinnamon, and more lemongrass. Simmer until the beef is nearly tender, then add carrots and continue cooking until soft. Serve with bread, rice noodles, or rice, garnished with herbs, onion, lime, and chili.

Soak wood ear mushrooms and cellophane noodles until softened, then mince or cut them small. Mix ground pork, minced shrimp, mushrooms, noodles, onion, jicama, eggs, garlic salt, pepper, sugar, sesame oil, and fish sauce. Place filling on wrappers, roll tightly, and seal. Fry in batches until golden brown and crisp. Serve with lettuce, herbs, and Vietnamese dipping sauce.

Poach chicken with aromatics until cooked, then cool and shred. Use the chicken broth to cook rice with turmeric for color and flavor. Toss the shredded chicken with onion, Vietnamese coriander, lime juice, salt, pepper, and chili. Serve the turmeric rice with the chicken salad, pickled vegetables, herbs, and a small bowl of broth.

Marinate pork belly slices and ground pork patties with fish sauce, sugar, shallots, garlic, pepper, and caramel sauce. Grill or pan-grill the pork until smoky and browned. Prepare the dipping broth with fish sauce, sugar, vinegar or lime juice, water, garlic, and chili. Add pickled green papaya or carrot to the sauce. Serve the grilled pork with rice vermicelli, lettuce, herbs, and warm dipping sauce.

Prepare the banh canh or udon noodles and toss with a little sesame oil. Sauté crab meat with annatto oil and minced garlic. Prepare shrimp and set aside. Simmer broth with seasonings until flavorful, then add noodles, crab meat, and shrimp. Serve hot with scallions, cilantro, fried shallots, black pepper, and chili oil if desired.

Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a medium pot over medium heat. Add minced garlic, sliced Thai chilies, and bruised lemongrass, then sauté for 10 to 20 seconds until fragrant. Add tamarind concentrate, fish sauce, and sugar, then stir well and bring to a boil. Add water, tomato wedges, and pineapple chunks. Bring back to a boil, then lower the heat, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes. Add the catfish steaks and simmer uncovered for about 10 minutes, skimming off any scum or impurities. Add bean sprouts and sliced taro stems, then turn off the heat so the residual heat cooks the vegetables. Serve hot topped with chopped rice paddy herb and fried shallots.
searchAliases: vietnamese sweet and sour fish soup
strMeasure8: 2 stalks, peeled and sliced on a bias

strInstructions: Cut the chicken into bite-sized pieces and marinate with fish sauce, sugar, chicken bouillon powder, black pepper, garlic, shallots, and sliced ginger. Heat oil in a pan, then add the marinated chicken and cook until the outside is lightly browned. Add caramel sauce or cook sugar until amber to create color, then stir the chicken until evenly coated. Add water or coconut soda if needed and simmer until the chicken is fully cooked and the sauce reduces into a glossy ginger caramel glaze. Finish with more black pepper and serve hot with steamed rice.

Cut beef into cubes and marinate with oyster sauce, soy sauce, fish sauce, sugar, garlic, and pepper. Prepare a dipping sauce with salt, pepper, and lime. Sear the beef in a very hot pan in batches so it browns quickly. Add onion and bell pepper if using, then toss until just cooked. Serve with tomato, lettuce, cucumber, and steamed rice or red tomato rice.

Cut firm tofu into large cubes and drain well, then deep-fry until golden and crisp. Boil or cook pork belly until tender, then slice into bite-sized pieces. Cook rice vermicelli and let it cool. Prepare the shrimp paste dipping sauce by mixing fermented shrimp paste with sugar, lime or calamansi juice, chili, and hot oil until foamy and balanced. Arrange the fried tofu, pork slices, vermicelli, cucumber, herbs, and dipping sauce on a platter. Serve immediately.
searchAliases: fermented shrimp paste with fried tofu

strInstructions: Marinate the pork belly with part of the annatto seed oil, chopped garlic, chopped red shallot, white pepper, chicken bouillon powder, fish sauce, and paprika for 20 minutes. Marinate the shrimp with the remaining seasoning at the same time. Heat oil in a pan and stir-fry the pork belly until mostly cooked, then add the shrimp and cook until both are cooked through. Let the filling cool. For the dough, combine tapioca starch, sugar, and salt in a large bowl. Pour in hot water gradually, kneading until a smooth playdough-like dough forms, then knead in oil. Divide the dough into small balls, flatten each piece, add about 1 teaspoon of pork and shrimp filling, then fold into a half-moon and seal tightly. Boil the dumplings in batches until they float and become translucent, then transfer them to cold water. Make scallion oil by pouring hot oil over chopped scallions mixed with salt and chicken bouillon powder. Serve the dumplings with scallion oil and Vietnamese dipping sauce.
strMeasure2: 120 g, deshelled and cut into 0.5 cm pieces
strMeasure5: 1/2 tbsp for filling plus 1 tsp for dough
strMeasure8: 1/2 tbsp for filling plus 3/4 tsp for scallion oil
strMeasure13: 1/2 tsp for dough plus 3/4 tsp for scallion oil
strMeasure14: 1 tbsp for dough plus 3 tbsp for filling plus 3/4 US cup for scallion oil

strInstructions: Make the batter with rice flour, corn starch, turmeric powder, water, coconut milk, salt, and green onions. Heat a banh khot pan with oil. Add batter to each mold, top with shrimp, cover, and cook until the edges are crisp and the centers are cooked. Brush with scallion oil and serve with lettuce, herbs, pickled vegetables, and Vietnamese dipping sauce.

Prepare the grilled pork paste by processing pork with garlic, fish sauce, sugar, salt, pepper, and baking powder until springy. Shape the mixture into logs or patties, then grill or broil until cooked through and lightly charred. Prepare rice paper, lettuce, herbs, cucumber, rice vermicelli, and crispy fried egg roll skins if using. Soften each rice paper sheet, then roll the grilled pork with the vegetables, herbs, noodles, and crispy pieces. Serve with a thick pork peanut dipping sauce or nuoc cham.
searchAliases: grilled pork paste rice paper rolls

strInstructions: Julienne the sweet potatoes. Make a batter with all-purpose flour, rice flour, salt, black pepper, chicken bouillon powder if using, turmeric powder, green onions, egg, and water. Mix the sweet potatoes into the batter. Add shrimp to each portion and deep-fry until golden brown and crisp. Serve with lettuce, herbs, pickled vegetables, and Vietnamese dipping sauce.

Snap or cut the water spinach into manageable pieces and wash thoroughly. Heat oil in a wok or skillet over high heat. Add lots of garlic and stir-fry until fragrant but not burned. Add the water spinach and toss quickly. Season with oyster sauce, sugar, and a little water if needed. Stir-fry until the stems are tender-crisp and the leaves are wilted. Serve immediately with rice.

Cut tofu into pieces and fry until golden, then set aside. In a pan, cook chopped tomatoes until softened and saucy. Add fish sauce, sugar, and seasoning, then return the fried tofu to the pan. Simmer briefly so the tofu absorbs the tomato sauce. Top with sliced scallions and serve hot with steamed rice.

Prepare sweetened red beans by cooking them until tender and mixing with sugar. Cook peeled split mung beans until soft, then mash into a paste with sugar. Prepare pandan jelly according to package directions and cut into strips. In a glass, layer red beans, mung bean paste, pandan jelly, crushed ice, and coconut sauce. Serve cold.

Prepare assorted canned and fresh fruits such as jackfruit, lychee, longan, palm seeds, and toddy palm. Make pandan jelly and cut it into strips. Mix coconut milk, milk or half-and-half, and sugar to make a creamy base. Combine the fruits and jelly in a large bowl, then pour in the creamy coconut mixture. Chill well and serve cold over ice.

Preheat the oven to 325°F. For the caramel, add sugar to a small stainless steel saucepan and pour water around the edge without stirring. Heat over medium heat until the sugar dissolves and caramelizes. Gently swirl the pan only if needed. Once the caramel turns dark amber, remove from heat and immediately divide it into ramekins. For the custard, heat whole milk, sugar, and salt until steaming but not boiling. In a bowl, gently mix eggs, egg yolks, and vanilla without beating in too much air. Slowly temper the eggs by adding the hot milk mixture one ladle at a time while stirring. Strain the custard through a sieve and pour it into the caramel-lined ramekins. Place the ramekins in a towel-lined baking dish, add hot water halfway up the ramekins, loosely cover with foil, and bake for 35 to 45 minutes until set. Cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 6 hours or overnight. To serve, run a knife around the ramekin edge and invert onto a plate.
strMeasure1: 3/4 cup for caramel plus 1/2 cup for custard

strInstructions: Simmer chicken with water, ginger, onion, and salt until cooked. Remove the chicken and shred the meat. Add rinsed rice to the broth and simmer until the grains break down into porridge. Season with fish sauce, salt, and pepper. Return some shredded chicken to the pot or serve it on top. Garnish with green onions, cilantro, fried shallots, black pepper, and ginger.

Warm coconut milk and sugar until the sugar dissolves, then let the mixture cool. Mix tapioca starch with rice flour and single-acting baking powder. Beat eggs gently, then combine with pandan extract and the cooled coconut milk mixture. Add the dry ingredients and mix until smooth, then strain the batter. Preheat the cake pan, pour in the batter, and bake until the cake rises, sets, and develops a honeycomb texture. Cool before slicing.

Clean the fish steaks and pat them dry. Marinate the fish with fish sauce, sugar, chicken bouillon powder, pepper, garlic, shallots, and chili. Heat oil in a claypot or heavy pan, then add the aromatics and fish. Add coconut water and caramel sauce, then simmer gently until the fish becomes tender and the sauce reduces into a thick, glossy glaze. Spoon the sauce over the fish while cooking. Finish with green onions, chili, and black pepper, then serve hot with steamed rice.

Clean the shrimp and cut the pork belly into small pieces. Marinate the pork and shrimp with fish sauce, sugar, chicken bouillon powder, pepper, garlic, and shallots. Cook the pork first until the fat begins to render, then add the shrimp and stir-fry until the shrimp turns orange. Add coconut water or water and simmer until the sauce thickens into a sticky caramel glaze. Continue tossing until the shrimp and pork are glossy. Garnish with green onions and serve with rice.

Cut the fish into large bite-sized pieces. Marinate with turmeric powder, galangal, garlic, shallots, fish sauce, shrimp paste, sugar, and oil. Let the fish absorb the marinade. Pan-fry or grill the fish until lightly browned and cooked through. Add lots of dill and scallions to the pan and toss briefly until just wilted. Serve with rice vermicelli, roasted peanuts, herbs, and dipping sauce.

Simmer chicken with water, onion, ginger, and salt until cooked, then remove the chicken and shred the meat. Prepare a clear broth with dried shrimp, dried squid if using, fish sauce, and rock sugar. Make thin egg crepes and slice them into strips. Prepare Vietnamese pork sausage, herbs, and rice vermicelli. Place noodles in bowls, then arrange shredded chicken, egg strips, pork sausage, herbs, and green onions on top. Ladle hot broth over everything and serve immediately.

Simmer chicken with water, ginger, onion, and salt until cooked. Remove the chicken, let it cool, and shred the meat. Season the broth with fish sauce, salt, and sugar. Soak glass noodles until pliable, then cook them briefly in hot broth or boiling water. Place the noodles in bowls and top with shredded chicken, herbs, fried shallots, green onions, and black pepper. Ladle the hot broth over the noodles and serve.

Clean pork hocks and simmer them with onion, dried shrimp, and water until the broth is rich and the pork is tender. Season the broth with fish sauce, salt, sugar, and chicken bouillon. Add shrimp near the end and cook until just done. Cook thick banh canh noodles separately or briefly in the broth. Serve the noodles with pork hock, shrimp, hot broth, green onions, cilantro, fried shallots, black pepper, and chili oil.
searchAliases: vietnamese pork hock thick noodle soup

strInstructions: Mix sugar, chicken powder, water, baking powder, potato starch, fish sauce, oil, pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder. Add the mixture to beef mince and mix thoroughly. Flatten the seasoned beef, wrap it tightly, and freeze until firm but still pliable. Process the chilled beef until it becomes a smooth paste. Shape into balls, then cook in hot water until bouncy and fully cooked. Serve in noodle soup or with dipping sauce.

Cook jicama and carrot until softened and lightly seasoned. Prepare thin egg strips by cooking beaten eggs into a thin omelette, then slicing. Prepare Chinese sausage, dried shrimp, lettuce, herbs, and rice paper. Soften a rice paper sheet, then layer lettuce, herbs, jicama-carrot filling, egg strips, sausage, and dried shrimp. Roll tightly and serve with hoisin peanut dipping sauce.
searchAliases: fresh spring rolls with jicama and egg

strInstructions: Prepare the vegetables by cutting potatoes, sweet potatoes, carrots, taro, and mushrooms into bite-sized pieces. Fry or pan-sear the tofu until golden. Sauté lemongrass, curry powder, garlic, and shallots until fragrant. Add the vegetables, coconut water or vegetable stock, and coconut milk. Simmer until the vegetables are tender. Add tofu near the end and season with salt, sugar, and vegetarian seasoning. Serve with baguette, rice, or noodles.

Cut rice paper into strips. Prepare the scallion oil and fried shallots. In a large bowl, combine rice paper strips, sate chili oil, green mango, Vietnamese coriander, peanuts, dried beef or dried shrimp if using, and hard-boiled eggs. Toss until the rice paper softens slightly and everything is evenly coated. Add more sate, salt, or kumquat juice to taste. Serve immediately as a street-food-style snack.

Rinse the rice until the water runs mostly clear. Heat oil in a pan and sauté garlic until fragrant. Add tomato paste and cook until the oil turns red. Add the rice and stir until each grain is coated with the tomato mixture. Transfer to a rice cooker or pot, add water or stock, and cook until fluffy. Let the rice rest, then fluff before serving.

Soak and cook split mung beans until soft, then mash them with sugar and coconut cream to form a smooth filling. Shape the filling into small balls. Mix glutinous rice flour with warm water to form a soft dough, then wrap the dough around each mung bean ball. Boil the dumplings until they float. Make ginger syrup with water, sugar, and sliced ginger. Serve the dumplings in the warm syrup with coconut cream and sesame seeds.

Prepare the filling by cooking ground pork with shallots, onion, wood ear mushrooms, fish sauce, pepper, and seasoning until savory. Prepare the rice flour batter by cooking rice flour, tapioca starch, stock, oil, and seasoning over low heat until thick and smooth. Soften banana leaves, then form them into cones. Add batter, pork filling, and quail egg, then cover with more batter. Wrap into pyramid shapes and steam until set. Serve warm with Vietnamese dipping sauce.

Simmer the chicken with ginger, star anise, cinnamon, garlic, soy sauces, salt, chicken bouillon powder, honey, and water until partly cooked and infused with flavor. Remove the chicken and let the skin dry thoroughly. Brush with annatto oil and lemon juice mixture to help color and crisp the skin. Deep-fry the chicken until the skin becomes golden and crispy while the meat stays juicy. Rest briefly before serving.

Soak glass noodles and wood ear mushrooms until softened, then chop them. Mix ground pork with eggs, noodles, mushrooms, onion, fish sauce, sugar, salt, and pepper. Transfer the mixture to a dish and steam until mostly set. Brush the top with beaten egg yolk, then continue steaming uncovered until the surface turns golden and glossy. Slice and serve with rice, cucumber, and dipping sauce.

Clean the prawns and trim if needed. Marinate with fish sauce, sugar, chicken bouillon powder, pepper, garlic, shallots, and oil. Heat a pan and cook the prawns until they turn orange. Add the marinade and simmer until the sauce thickens into a sweet-salty glaze. Continue tossing until the prawns are glossy and coated. Garnish with green onions and serve with steamed rice.

Soak and cook split mung beans until soft, then mash with sugar to form a sweet filling. Shape the filling into balls. Mix glutinous rice flour, rice flour, sugar, mashed potato or potato flakes if using, and warm water into a smooth dough. Wrap dough around each mung bean ball, seal well, and roll in sesame seeds. Deep-fry slowly until the sesame balls are golden, puffed, and crisp. Drain before serving.

Slice or flatten ripe bananas. Prepare the coconut cream mixture with coconut milk, condensed milk or sugar, and pandan if using. Layer bananas with the coconut mixture in a container or plastic bag. Sprinkle roasted peanuts and shredded coconut between the layers. Freeze until firm, then cut into bars or serve as frozen banana coconut ice cream.

Separate and wash mustard greens, then dry them in the sun or air-dry until slightly wilted. Cut the greens into pieces and combine with onion. Make a brine with warm water, salt, sugar, and vinegar if using. Pack the vegetables into a clean jar and pour in the brine until submerged. Let ferment at room temperature until sour and crunchy. Refrigerate once the flavor is ready.

Peel and cut carrots and daikon into thin matchsticks. Sprinkle with salt and knead briefly to draw out moisture, then rinse and drain. Make the pickling liquid by dissolving sugar in warm water, then adding vinegar. Pack the carrots and daikon into a clean jar and pour in the pickling liquid. Let the vegetables pickle until tangy and crisp. Use in banh mi, rice plates, noodle bowls, or rolls.
